title: DeepSeekMoE: Towards Ultimate Expert Specialization in Mixture-of-Experts Language Models
key_features: Addresses knowledge hybridity in existing MoE architectures
Addresses knowledge redundancy in existing MoE architectures

performance_comparisons.result: Comparable performance with only about 40% of computations
performance_comparisons.note: LLaMA2 7B has approximately 2.5 times the activated parameters

performance_comparisons.result: Comparable performance with only 28.5% (possibly 18.2%) of computations

issues_addressed.description: Existing MoE practices often employ a limited number of experts, causing tokens assigned to a specific expert to cover diverse knowledge
Tokens assigned to different experts may require common knowledge, leading to redundancy in expert parameters

evaluation.figure: Figure 1 shows comparison between DeepSeekMoE 16B and other open source models

strategies: Two principal strategies to address knowledge hybridity and redundancy
Details are truncated in the provided content
